spec_match.rationale: The concept is outstanding, linking client-side form submissions to live JWT generation and showing the specific headers (Sec-Email-Verification-Nonce) in detail.

interactive.rationale: Extremely interactive, featuring a 4-step wizard that consumes custom input text, splits signed JWT anatomy dynamically, and lets developers simulate replay protection blocks.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfectly represents the entire lifecycle of browser-mediated OTP replacement.

edge_cases.rationale: The fallback section now feature-detects the real EVP page surface, onemailverified on email inputs, instead of the former fake navigator-level verification API.

references.rationale: Correctly cites the parent ChromeStatus reference 5205725253074944 in the footer see-also block.

design_system.rationale: Highly conformant, featuring responsive column grids, clean monospace layouts, and high-contrast borders.

openQuestions.title: Re-review repaired live probe in chrome-devtools-mcp
openQuestions.detail: The live probe or copyable feature-detection snippet was repaired on 2026-06-04. Re-run this route in chrome-devtools-mcp, exercise the visible controls, and verify the repaired probe output plus console/network state before marking the critique fully current.

summary: The verification-flow-demo is a premier developer demonstration of the Email Verification Protocol. It provides excellent interactive step-by-step states, header inspect panels, and a replay-attack validation matrix. 2026-06-04 maintenance replaced inaccurate live-probe logic with feature-specific checks; chrome-devtools-mcp was unavailable in this session, so browser click-through re-review is still required.
